mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.5%, it signals a labor market recovery that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 7.5% in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and residential investment indicate synchronized demand expansion across construction and durable goods sectors, which historically precedes broad equity outperformance within one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero predictive power for 20-day returns across two consecutive rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding factory output and capacity utilization, which directly improves corporate profit margins and drives short-term equity gains during periods of stable inflation.
regime_hint: stable inflation environment with CPIAUCSL monthly growth below 0.4%
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises alongside CPIAUCSL monthly growth exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust aggregate demand, which historically supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than half of all instances where quarterly GDP growth surpasses 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth above 150,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which reliably fuels consumer spending and lifts equity valuations within a month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when payroll growth exceeds 150k but unemployment remains above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation increases input costs and compresses real corporate margins, leading to immediate negative pressure on equity valuations over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations despite rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly increase in housing starts exceeding 50 units signals strengthening construction demand and household formation, which historically drives positive equity returns over the next quarter through wealth effects and supply chain activity.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where HOUST increases by >50 units over three months.
